title: aconitum napellus f. boreale
intro: Aconitum napellus f. boreale, also known as the monk's hood or Aconitum boreale, is a subspecies of the Aconitum napellus plant, a perennial flowering plant native to the northern hemisphere. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, antipyretic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its alkaloid content, including aconitine, aconitine-like compounds, and other glycosides. The medicinal properties of Aconitum napellus f. boreale have been associated with various health benefits, including pain relief, reduced fever, and potential anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used to make tinctures, decoctions, and infusions, although caution is advised due to its toxicity and potential side effects.
